metadata.name: pygame-core
metadata.summary: A modular core for Pygame-based 2D games.

modules.name: core.utils
documentation: This module contains utility functions that are used in the game.

functions.name: core.utils.check_collision
documentation:
Check if two rectangles are colliding.

Args:
    rect1 (pygame.Rect): The first rectangle.
    rect2 (pygame.Rect): The second rectangle.

Returns:
    bool: True if the rectangles are colliding, False otherwise.

functions.name: core.utils.wrap_around_screen
documentation:
Wrap an entity around the screen if it goes off the edge.

Args:
    rect (pygame.Rect): The entity to wrap around the screen.
    screen_width (int): The width of the screen.
    screen_height (int): The height of the screen.

modules.name: core.scene
documentation: This module contains the Scene class

classes.name: core.scene.Scene

methods.name: core.scene.Scene.update
documentation:
Updates the scene. Override in subclasses.

Args:
    input_manager (InputManager): The input manager to query input states.

methods.name: core.scene.Scene.render
documentation: Renders the scene. Override in subclasses.
The Scene class is the base class for all scenes in the game.

modules.name: core.dataclasses
documentation: Dataclasses for the core module.

classes.name: core.dataclasses.Cords
documentation: A simple dataclass to store x and y coordinates.

classes.name: core.dataclasses.Size
documentation: A simple dataclass to store width and height.

modules.name: core
documentation: Core module for the game engine. Contains the main classes and utilities.

modules.name: core.game
documentation: Game module for handling the game loop and events.

classes.name: core.game.Game

methods.name: core.game.Game.run
documentation:
Runs the game loop with the specified scene.

Args:
    scene (Scene): The scene to run in the game loop.

methods.name: core.game.Game.handle_global_events
documentation: Handles global events such as quitting the game.

The Game class is responsible for handling the game loop and events.

Attributes:
    screen (pygame.Surface): The main game screen.
    clock (pygame.time.Clock): The game clock.
    fps (int): The target frames per second.
    running (bool): A flag to indicate if the game is running.

modules.name: core.input_manager
documentation: This module contains the InputManager class, which is responsible for handling input from the user.

classes.name: core.input_manager.InputManager

methods.name: core.input_manager.InputManager.update
documentation: Updates the state of all keys.

methods.name: core.input_manager.InputManager.is_pressed
documentation:
Returns True if the specified key is pressed, False otherwise.

Args:
    key (int): The key to check.

Returns:
    bool: True if the key is pressed, False otherwise.

The InputManager class is responsible for handling input from the user.

Attributes:
    keys (dict): A dictionary to store the state of all keys.

modules.name: core.entity
documentation: This module contains the Entity class, which is the base class for all entities in the game.

classes.name: core.entity.Entity

methods.name: core.entity.Entity.draw
documentation:
Draws the entity on the screen.

Args:
    screen (pygame.Surface): The surface to draw the entity on.

methods.name: core.entity.Entity.move
documentation:
Moves the entity by the specified amount in the x and y directions.

Args:
    dx (int): The amount to move the entity in the x direction.
    dy (int): The amount to move the entity in the y direction.

The Entity class is the base
class for all entities in the game.

Attributes:
    rect (pygame.Rect): The rectangular area that the entity occupies.
    color (tuple): The color of the entity.

Methods:
    draw(screen):
        Draws the entity on the screen.
    move(dx, dy):
        Moves the entity by the specified amount in the x and y directions.

modules.name: core.scene_manager
documentation: This module contains the SceneManager class.

classes.name: core.scene_manager.SceneManager

methods.name: core.scene_manager.SceneManager.update
documentation:
Updates the current scene with input states.

Args:
    input_manager (InputManager): The input manager to query input states.

methods.name: core.scene_manager.SceneManager.render
documentation: Renders the current scene.

The SceneManager class is responsible for managing the scenes in the game.

Attributes:
    current_scene (Scene): The current scene that is being run.

Methods:
    __init__(initial_scene):
        Initializes the SceneManager with the specified initial scene.
    update(input_manager):
        Updates the current scene with input states.
    render():
        Renders the current scene.

modules.name: core.settings
documentation: Settings for the game.

classes.name: core.settings.Settings
documentation: A class to store all settings for the game.
pylint: disable=too-few-public-methods

modules.name: core.asset_manager
documentation:
The asset_manager module contains the AssetManager class, which is a utility class
for loading and caching game assets such as images and sounds.

classes.name: core.asset_manager.AssetManager

methods.name: core.asset_manager.AssetManager.load_image
documentation:
Loads an image from the specified path and caches it.
If the image is already cached, returns the cached image.

Args:
    path (str): The file path to the image.

Returns:
    pygame.Surface: The loaded image.

methods.name: core.asset_manager.AssetManager.load_sound
documentation:
Loads a sound from the specified path and caches it.
If the sound is already cached, returns the cached sound.

Args:
    path (str): The file path to the sound.

Returns:
    pygame.mixer.Sound: The loaded sound.

AssetManager is a utility class for loading and caching game assets such as images and sounds.

Attributes:
    _cache (dict): A dictionary to store cached assets to avoid reloading them multiple times.

Methods:
    load_image(path):
        Loads an image from the specified path and caches it.
        If the image is already cached, returns the cached image.
    load_sound(path):
        Loads a sound from the specified path and caches it.
        If the sound is already cached, returns the cached sound.
